description.en: Dive into multi-head self-attention — the core operation of Transformers — and understand how Q, K, V projections enable each token to attend to every other token.

story_variants.en:
## Self-Attention: Q, K, V Matrices

In **self-attention**, the queries, keys, and values all come from the **same sequence**. Every token attends to every other token — letting the model capture long-range dependencies in a single operation.

### Projecting to Q, K, V

Given input embeddings `X` (shape: [seq_len, d_model]), we create Q, K, V by linear projections:

```python
W_Q = np.random.randn(d_model, d_k)  # (512, 64)
W_K = np.random.randn(d_model, d_k)  # (512, 64)
W_V = np.random.randn(d_model, d_v)  # (512, 64)

Q = X @ W_Q   # (seq_len, d_k)
K = X @ W_K   # (seq_len, d_k)
V = X @ W_V   # (seq_len, d_v)
```

### Multi-Head Attention

Instead of one set of Q/K/V, use **h heads** in parallel — each head can attend to different relationships:

```
head_i = Attention(Q·W_Qi, K·W_Ki, V·W_Vi)
MultiHead = Concat(head_1, ..., head_h) · W_O
```

| GPT-2 Small | Value |
|------------|-------|
| d_model | 768 |
| n_heads | 12 |
| d_k per head | 64 |
| n_layers | 12 |

### Attention Mask

For **causal** (decoder) self-attention, we mask future positions so the model cannot look ahead:

```python
mask = np.tril(np.ones((seq_len, seq_len)))  # lower triangle
scores = scores * mask + (1 - mask) * (-1e9) # set upper to -inf
weights = softmax(scores)  # masked positions get weight ≈ 0
```

### Positional Encoding

> **Key Concept:** Self-attention has no notion of position — the same output regardless of word order. **Positional encodings** are added to input embeddings to inject order information. Transformers use sine/cosine waves at different frequencies.

```python
PE[pos, 2i]   = sin(pos / 10000^(2i/d_model))
PE[pos, 2i+1] = cos(pos / 10000^(2i/d_model))
```

steps.line: Q = X @ W_Q  # project to query space

explanation.en: Each row asks: 'what am I looking for?'

steps.line: K = X @ W_K  # project to key space

explanation.en: Each row says: 'I contain this information'

steps.line: V = X @ W_V  # project to value space

explanation.en: Each row says: 'here is my content'
Every token compares with every other token: O(n²) complexity
Weighted blend of all value vectors

instruction.en: Implement `causal_mask(seq_len)` that returns a lower-triangular boolean matrix of shape `(seq_len, seq_len)`. Positions where mask is False should be set to -1e9 in the attention scores (not attended to).

hints.en: Use np.tril(np.ones((n, n), dtype=bool)) for the lower triangle
Position (i,j) should be True if j <= i (can see current and past tokens)
For a 4-token sequence: row 0 sees only token 0, row 3 sees tokens 0-3

practice_challenges.test_code:
import numpy as np
mask = causal_mask(4)
assert mask.shape == (4, 4), f'Expected (4,4), got {mask.shape}'
assert mask[0, 0] == True, 'Token 0 should see itself'
assert mask[0, 1] == False, 'Token 0 should NOT see token 1'
assert mask[3, 0] == True, 'Token 3 can see token 0'
assert mask[3, 3] == True, 'Token 3 can see itself'
assert np.sum(mask) == 4*5//2, 'Lower triangle has n*(n+1)/2 True values'
